report: C6 — IPS / Snort 3 -> Rapid Threat Containment (Stage C)

versions.snort_engine: SNORT3 / PREVENTION, base Connectivity Over Security

ise35.role: PSN — policy decision point + CoA origin (UDP 1700) + pxGrid controller

FTDv.health: yellow (cosmetic; MANAGED/DEPLOYED, dropped the trigger + raised the event)

trigger: printf 'C6TRIGGER probe\n' | nc -w 3 198.18.128.51 8000  (x2, raw payload -> stays in pkt_data)

cases.objective: Custom Snort 3 rule exists in FMC (Local Rules, gid 2000 sid 1000001)

evidence.ruleData: alert tcp any any -> any any ( msg:"LOCAL C6 RTC test trigger"; flow:to_server,established; content:"C6TRIGGER",nocase; sid:1000001; gid:2000; rev:5; )

cases.objective: IPS policy SDA-IPS attached to the allow rule Permit-CAMPUS-Services (Snort inspects the flow)

evidence.ips_policy: SDA-IPS inspectionMode=PREVENTION, basePolicy='Connectivity Over Security' (active base -> compiles Local Rules; gotcha #1)
evidence.on_box: FTDv 'show access-control-config' -> Rule: Permit-CAMPUS-Services / Action: Allow / Intrusion Policy: SDA-IPS ; Source net-campus10 (172.16.10.0/24) -> Dest host-splunk (198.18.128.51) among others

cases.objective: Baseline (uncontained): 1 active session, no ANC, HOST1->host-splunk 0% loss

evidence.ise_session: alice active, Employees SGT 4, PermitAccess, rule Employees_SGT, PEAP(EAP-MSCHAPv2), store mitchcloud, NAS 10.1.0.3 (EDGE1.lab.local) Gi1/0/3, audit-session 0217010A000000146FFB1A92

cases.objective: Fire C6TRIGGER -> FTD Snort drops it (Blocked Packets increments)

evidence.note: the first data segment elicited an HTTP 400 from Splunkd yet Blocked Packets still incremented and the intrusion event was raised (drives the RTC loop) — the auto-quarantine outcome is unambiguous

cases.objective: IPS drop auto-quarantines alice via the RTC loop (ANC + CoA + containment) — no human

evidence.auto_anc: NO ise_apply_anc called; ANC endpoints [] -> 2 records, both macAddress 52:54:00:03:0B:0D / policyName Quarantine (ids de1dea5c-06c7-43af-89ea-4b95a4c2bf11, 8434eee2-147e-4adc-a05a-972fcb03324b)
evidence.coa: MnT AuthStatus msg 5205 'Dynamic Authorization succeeded', response {Error-Cause=200}, 2026-07-17T12:37:25.647Z, audit-session 0217010A000000146FFB1A92 (alice's live session); followed by 5417/11103 client-side termination
evidence.edge1: show access-session int Gi1/0/3 -> 'No sessions match supplied criteria'

cases.objective: Reversibility: clear ANC + reassociate -> session restored, no residual, 0% loss

evidence.clear: ise_clear_anc(52:54:00:03:0B:0D, Quarantine) x2 (one per auto-created record) -> ANC endpoints []
evidence.reassociate: HOST1 'sudo -n wpa_cli -i eth0 reassociate' -> OK; wpa_state=COMPLETED, PAE=AUTHENTICATED, suppPortStatus=Authorized, EAP=SUCCESS
evidence.edge1: Gi1/0/3 Authorized, User alice, VRF CAMPUS_VN, IPv4 172.16.10.50, SGT Value 4, new session 0217010A000000157016CAEF
evidence.ping_198.18.128.51: 0% loss (5/5) after ~90s LISP EID re-registration (control plane restored within seconds; data path returned after a further reconvergence cycle)

observations: Two ANC records were auto-created for the same MAC (two triggers -> two correlation fires; ISE ERS permits duplicate ancendpoint records for a MAC). Required two ise_clear_anc calls to fully clear. A single trigger creates one record. Containment is identical; not a defect.

The trigger's first data segment drew an HTTP 400 from Splunkd (port 8000) yet the Snort Blocked-Packets counter still incremented and the intrusion event fired the RTC loop — consistent with the pkt_data/http-buffer nuance (gotcha #2). The definitive outcome (auto-quarantine + 100% loss) is unambiguous.
LISP EID re-registration took ~90s for the data path to return this run (longer than the prior ~15-30s); the control plane (EDGE1 Authorized/SGT 4, ISE live session) restored within seconds.
FTDv health reported 'yellow' (cosmetic) but MANAGED/DEPLOYED and fully functional — it dropped the trigger and raised the event.
gotchas: 'No Rules Active' base does NOT compile custom Local Rules -> use an active base (Connectivity Over Security) so the rule group compiles and the per-rule DROP override takes effect.
HTTP-inspected flows move the payload into HTTP buffers (a bare content on pkt_data won't match) -> use a raw non-HTTP nc payload (reliable) or the http_uri sticky buffer.
Correlate on Rule SID (1000001), not Generator ID — FMC files user rules under GID 2000, which is not in the correlation Generator-ID picker.
reversibility: no built config modified; only reversible ANC round-trips + trigger traffic, all undone; alice verified fully restored (SGT 4, 0% loss, no ANC residual, 1 active session).
